Idiomatic Expressions

  • 公念生无罪 - 公 refers to a high-ranking official or magistrate. 念 means to think or consider. 生 means a person or individual. 无罪 means innocent. The phrase suggests that the official considered the person to be innocent.
  • 几陷大辟 - 几 means almost or nearly. 陷 means to fall into or be involved in. 大辟 is an ancient form of capital punishment. The phrase means almost being sentenced to death.
  • 诃责邑宰及承讯各官 - 诃责 means to rebuke or scold. 邑宰 refers to a local magistrate. 承讯各官 refers to officials involved in the case. The phrase means rebuking the local magistrate and the officials involved in the case.
  • 使为媒 - 使 means to make or cause. 为媒 means to act as a matchmaker. The phrase means to arrange the marriage.
  • 以珊柯妻生 - 以 means to use or employ. 珊柯 is a name. 妻 means to marry. 生 is the person being married. The phrase means to marry Shan Ke to the person.
  • 并罚醵资助奁，资生膏火 - 并罚 means to impose penalties. 醵资 means to raise funds. 助奁 means to help with the dowry. 资生膏火 means to provide for the person's living expenses. The phrase means to impose penalties, raise funds, and help with the dowry and living expenses.
  • 以赎其愆 - 以 means to use or employ. 赎 means to atone or redeem. 其 refers to the person. 愆 means fault or wrongdoing. The phrase means to atone for the person's fault.
  • 闻者无不啧啧称颂 - 闻者 means those who hear or know about it. 无不 means all without exception. 啧啧 means to praise enthusiastically. 称颂 means to praise or commend. The phrase means that everyone who heard about it praised it enthusiastically.
  • 公始悟梦兆铜镜掷碎其半者 - 公始悟 means the official finally understood. 梦兆 means a dream omen. 铜镜 means a bronze mirror. 掷碎 means to throw and break. 其半者 means half of it. The phrase means the official finally understood the dream omen of the bronze mirror being thrown and broken in half.
  • 仅存二金字 - 仅存 means only remains. 二金字 means two golden characters. The phrase means only two golden characters remain.
  • 月合半明，为朋字，统合之，金二朋即灭也 - 月合半明 means the moon is half bright. 为朋字 means it forms the character for 'friendship' (朋). 统合之 means to combine them. 金二朋即灭也 means two golden characters of friendship will be extinguished. The phrase means the moon being half bright forms the character for 'friendship,' and when combined, two golden characters of friendship will be extinguished.
  • 后二语，谓生与珊柯当为夫妻，先有缺陷，而后团圆 - 后二语 means the last two phrases. 谓 means to say or state. 生与珊柯 means the person and Shan Ke. 当为夫妻 means should be husband and wife. 先有缺陷 means there was a defect at first. 而后团圆 means and then they would be reunited. The phrase means the last two phrases say that the person and Shan Ke should be husband and wife, but there was a defect at first and then they would be reunited.
  • 然必先暂使团圆，而后乃无缺陷 - 然 means however or but. 必 means must. 先暂使团圆 means to temporarily reunite them first. 而后乃无缺陷 means and then there will be no defects. The phrase means however, they must be temporarily reunited first, and then there will be no defects.
  • 公初授计狱吏 - 公初 means the official initially. 授计 means to be assigned or given a task. 狱吏 means a prison official. The phrase means the official was initially assigned to a prison official.
  • 固是揣度结句而有所触 - 固是 means indeed or certainly. 揣度 means to speculate or guess. 结句 means the concluding phrase. 而有所触 means and had a realization. The phrase means indeed, the official speculated on the concluding phrase and had a realization.
  • 不谓果以此而获真犯也 - 不谓 means did not expect. 果 means indeed or actually. 以此 means for this reason. 而获真犯也 means and caught the real culprit. The phrase means did not expect that indeed, for this reason, the real culprit was caught.
  • 生出狱后，喜妻珊柯 - 生出狱后 means the person after being released from prison. 喜妻 means to be happy with the wife. 珊柯 is the wife's name. The phrase means the person, after being released from prison, was happy with his wife Shan Ke.
  • 又得助醵赀 - 又得助 means also received help. 醵赀 means to raise funds. The phrase means also received help in raising funds.
  • 益发奋下帷，寻联捷 - 益发奋 means even more diligent. 下帷 means to study intensively. 寻联捷 means soon achieved success. The phrase means even more diligent in studying and soon achieved success.
  • 入词馆，秩跻清要 - 入词馆 means entered the literary bureau. 秩跻 means advanced in rank. 清要 means a high and important position. The phrase means entered the literary bureau and advanced to a high and important position.
  • 士林传为美谈，足征学使品藻不谬 - 士林 means the scholarly community. 传为美谈 means passed down as a beautiful story. 足征 means sufficient to prove. 学使 means the provincial examiner. 品藻 means evaluation. 不谬 means not mistaken. The phrase means the scholarly community passed it down as a beautiful story, sufficient to prove that the provincial examiner's evaluation was not mistaken.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 想业由前世，夫复何说！” This opening rhetorical question sets the tone for the narrative, suggesting that the current situation is a result of past actions.
  • 诘得食鸡一事，便托腹肌，出钱命代市一鸡，倩人烹好，即置于向日子所具食之处，乃一葡萄架下. This long sentence describes a series of actions taken by the official, including an investigation, a request for a chicken, and its preparation and placement.
  • 公留心黠察，见热气上薰，少选，架上一丝下缒，直入碗中，非竭目力不见. This sentence describes the official's careful observation and the discovery of a mysterious thread.
  • 公知有异，取一脔饲犬，犬毙.乃谓妪曰：“尔妇之冤，我能代申，尔姑待之可也.” This sequence describes the official's suspicion, the test with the dog, and his promise to the old woman.
  • 公将熟鸡裹以旋署，檄邑令及承讯在事各官至，以实告之，众喏喏相视，若不深信. This sequence describes the official's actions to resolve the matter, including summoning officials and telling them the truth.
  • 命人往搜架上，得一蝎，长四寸许，盖所缒之丝即是物也. This sequence describes the discovery of the scorpion and the conclusion that the thread was indeed the scorpion.
  • 公乃以实入告，自请议处，邑令以诬拟罪论抵，余各议罚有差. This sequence describes the official's report to the emperor, his voluntary punishment, and the varied penalties imposed on others.
  • 又请以贤孝旌某氏妇，以义士旌十郎，各建坊以慰冤魂；妪着地方有司伏恤，以终余年.制曰：“可。” This sequence describes the official's requests for honors and care, and the emperor's approval.
  • 此狱幸赖中丞之贤，卒得暴白.向使幕宾因居停来问，一经诘驳，断不致令贤妇义士抱屈九泉.观其暮夜叩门索命一节，洵属快事.可不慎欤！可不慎欤！ This closing sequence reflects on the case, the virtues of the vice-premier, and the importance of careful action.

  • 手谈 - Term for playing chess, often used metaphorically to denote intellectual or strategic discussions.

  • 百喙 - Metaphor for 'many mouths' or 'innumerable arguments,' used to describe futile attempts to defend oneself.
  • 归正首邱 - Metaphor from the *Book of Songs* meaning 'to return to one's original place,' symbolizing a desire to return home or to one's roots.
  • 孝心 - Refers to filial piety, a central virtue in Confucian thought.
  • 哀矜而勿喜 - Phrase from the *Analects* meaning 'to feel compassion and pity without rejoicing,' emphasizing the importance of empathy in judgment.
  • 罪疑惟轻，功疑惟重 - Principle of criminal law from the *Book of History* meaning 'in cases of doubt, err on the side of leniency in punishment and generosity in reward.'
  • 与其杀不辜，宁失不经 - Principle from the *Book of History* meaning 'it is better to err in favor of the innocent than to follow an unjust law.'
  • 存方寸 - Metaphor for maintaining a clear and just mind, often associated with the heart or conscience.
  • 子惠元元 - Phrase meaning 'to benefit the common people,' emphasizing the moral duty of rulers to care for their subjects.

  • 幕宾 - Refers to a retainer or advisor in the official's entourage, often a scholar or legal expert.

  • 初固料不及此 - Phrase meaning 'initially did not expect this'; '初固' emphasizes the initial expectation and '料不及此' means 'did not anticipate this.'
  • 形迹可疑 - Phrase meaning 'suspicious behavior'; '形迹' refers to outward behavior or appearance and '可疑' means suspicious.
  • 彼便无故亡去 - Phrase meaning 'he suddenly disappeared without cause'; '无故' means 'without reason' and '亡去' means 'disappeared.'
  • 安知非畏罪而遁 - Rhetorical question meaning 'how can it not be that he fled out of fear of guilt?'; '安知非' is a rhetorical construction meaning 'how can it not be' and '畏罪而遁' means 'fled out of fear of guilt.'
  • 爰以情告尚书 - Phrase meaning 'therefore, I reported the situation to the Minister of Justice'; '爰' means 'therefore' and '尚书' refers to the Minister of Justice.
  • 拘送有司 - Phrase meaning 'detained and sent to the authorities'; '有司' refers to the relevant officials or authorities.
  • 一械尽吐其实 - Phrase meaning 'under interrogation, he fully confessed'; '一械' refers to the use of force or pressure and '尽吐其实' means 'fully revealed the truth.'
  • 闽抚以师无罪 - Phrase meaning 'the governor of Fujian declared the teacher innocent'; '闽抚' refers to the governor of Fujian and '无罪' means 'innocent.'
  • 具文咨准开复 - Phrase meaning 'prepared a formal document to request and obtain reinstatement'; '具文' means 'prepared a formal document,' '咨准' means 'requested and obtained approval,' and '开复' means 'reinstatement.'
  • 并将整容匠槛解回籍 - Phrase meaning 'and ordered the embalmer to be shackled and sent back to his home'; '整容匠' refers to the embalmer, '槛解' means 'shackled and sent,' and '回籍' means 'returned to his hometown.'
  • 讯质定罪 - Phrase meaning 'questioned and sentenced'; '讯质' means 'questioned' and '定罪' means 'sentenced.'
  • 此狱已越六七年 - Phrase meaning 'this case has lasted for six or seven years'; '狱' means 'case' or 'trial' and '越' means 'has passed.'
  • 某秀才正以恸子卧病 - Phrase meaning 'a certain scholar was grieving over his son and was ill'; '恸子' means 'grieving over his son' and '卧病' means 'was ill.'
  • 深虑行踪败露 - Phrase meaning 'deeply worried that his movements would be exposed'; '深虑' means 'deeply worried' and '行踪败露' means 'movements exposed.'
  • 谋与师联名上控 - Phrase meaning 'planned to join the teacher in filing a joint appeal'; '联名上控' means 'filed a joint appeal.'
  • 务求昭雪 - Phrase meaning 'determined to seek a clear exoneration'; '昭雪' means 'clear exoneration.'
  • 前邑令已荐擢郡守 - Phrase meaning 'the former magistrate had been promoted to governor'; '荐擢' means 'promoted' and '郡守' refers to the governor.
  • 倩人关说 - Phrase meaning 'hired someone to intercede'; '倩人' means 'hired someone' and '关说' means 'interceded.'
  • 以重资啖二人 - Phrase meaning 'offered a large sum of money to the two people'; '重资' means 'a large sum of money' and '啖' means 'offered.'
  • 求罢控 - Phrase meaning 'asked to drop the appeal'; '罢控' means 'drop the appeal.'
  • 初固不许 - Phrase meaning 'initially refused'; '初固' emphasizes the initial refusal.
  • 既念死者不能复生 - Phrase meaning 'but then thought that the dead could not be brought back to life'; '既念' means 'but then thought' and '死者不能复生' means 'the dead could not be brought back to life.'
  • 又贪重资 - Phrase meaning 'and also coveted the large sum of money'; '贪' means 'coveted' and '重资' means 'large sum of money.'
  • 遂不复控 - Phrase meaning 'and so dropped the appeal'; '遂' means 'and so' and '不复控' means 'dropped the appeal.'
  • 惟将整容匠瘐死狱中 - Phrase meaning 'only the embalmer died in prison'; '瘐死' means 'died in prison' and '狱中' means 'in prison.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 闽 - Refers to Fujian, a province in southeastern China.
  • 师 - Refers to the teacher or a person of authority.
  • 尚书 - Refers to the Minister of Justice, a high-ranking official in the imperial bureaucracy.
  • 有司 - Refers to the relevant officials or authorities responsible for handling legal matters.
  • 闽抚 - Refers to the governor of Fujian.
  • 郡守 - Refers to the governor of a prefecture.
  • 秀才 - Refers to a scholar or a person who has passed the lowest level of the imperial examination.
  • 邑令 - Refers to the magistrate of a county.
  • 整容匠 - Refers to the embalmer, a person responsible for preparing bodies for burial.
  • 狱 - Refers to a case or trial in the legal system.
  • 上控 - Refers to the act of filing an appeal to a higher authority.
  • 昭雪 - Refers to a clear exoneration or acquittal.
  • 关说 - Refers to the practice of interceding or lobbying to influence decisions.
  • 重资 - Refers to a large sum of money, often used as a bribe.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 转徙至闽，不图忽与师遇，深虑行踪败露，故心虚情怯，猝见师，急欲回避 - This sentence describes a series of actions and reactions, where the subject (likely a person) migrates to Fujian, unexpectedly meets the teacher, becomes worried about being exposed, feels timid and fearful, and urgently wants to avoid the teacher.
  • 师初固料不及此，今见其形迹可疑，因忆此狱初兴时，彼便无故亡去，安知非畏罪而遁 - This sentence explains the teacher's initial lack of expectation, followed by the observation of suspicious behavior, and the memory of the person's sudden disappearance, leading to the suspicion that the person fled out of fear of guilt.
  • 爰以情告尚书，拘送有司，果一械尽吐其实 - This sentence describes the teacher's action of reporting the situation to the Minister of Justice, resulting in the person being detained and questioned, and ultimately confessing under pressure.
  • 闽抚以师无罪，具文咨准开复 - This sentence describes the governor of Fujian declaring the teacher innocent and preparing a formal document to request and obtain reinstatement.
  • 并将整容匠槛解回籍，讯质定罪 - This sentence describes the order to shackle the embalmer and send him back to his home, followed by questioning and sentencing.

  • 致使无辜儒童含冤地下 - '致使' means 'causing,' '无辜儒童' means 'innocent scholar child,' and '含冤地下' means 'suffering injustice in the underworld.'
  • 可谓憯极 - '可谓' means 'can be called,' and '憯极' means 'extremely cruel' or 'most pitiful.'
  • 然天道之巧 - '然' means 'however,' and '天道之巧' means 'the ingenious workings of heaven.'
  • 其师议流 - '其师' means 'the teacher,' and '议流' means 'decided to exile.'
  • 不于他处而在闽省 - '不于他处' means 'not elsewhere,' and '闽省' means 'Fujian province.'
  • 俾与真犯相遇 - '俾' means 'to make,' and '与真犯相遇' means 'to meet the real culprit.'
  • 事越六七年 - '事越' means 'the matter passed,' and '六七年' means 'six to seven years.'
  • 卒使覆盆得雪 - '卒使' means 'finally made,' '覆盆' is a metaphor for injustice, and '得雪' means 'cleared' or 'exonerated.'
  • 诚属快事 - '诚属' means 'truly belongs to,' and '快事' means 'a joyful matter.'
  • 惟师与某秀才，但贪重资，不肯联名上控 - '惟师与某秀才' means 'only the teacher and a certain scholar,' '但贪重资' means 'but greedy for a large sum of money,' and '不肯联名上控' means 'refused to file a joint appeal.'
  • 不然县令虽擢郡守，焉能逃罪哉 - '不然' means 'otherwise,' '县令虽擢郡守' means 'even if the county magistrate was promoted to governor,' and '焉能逃罪哉' means 'how could he escape punishment?'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 翰林 - Refers to the Hanlin Academy, an elite institution in the imperial bureaucracy responsible for drafting important documents and advising the emperor.
  • 民社 - Refers to the local administration, specifically the county level.
  • 覆盆 - A metaphor for injustice, often used to describe a situation where the truth is obscured, like a bowl covering something.
  • 闽省 - Refers to Fujian province, a region in southeastern China.
  • 秀才 - Refers to a scholar who has passed the first level of the imperial examinations, indicating a certain level of education and social status.

  • 秀才 - A scholar who has passed the first level of the imperial examinations.

  • This sentence introduces the background of Du Mei returning to her parents' home and bringing Hui Juan with her.
  • This sentence describes the aftermath of the marriage discussion, where Lu forbids Hui Juan from visiting again.
  • This sentence describes another instance where You Mei visits, but Lu still prevents Hui Juan from seeing him.
  • This sentence describes the deep longing between You Mei and Hui Juan after they are separated.
  • This sentence describes the passage of time and the growing romantic feelings between You Mei and Hui Juan.
  • This sentence describes the mutual longing between You Mei and Hui Juan, who can only find comfort in themselves and cannot share their true feelings openly.
  • This sentence describes You Mei's actions in sending a painting and a poem to Hui Juan to gauge her feelings.
  • This sentence is a compliment to You Mei, praising his talent and charm.
  • This sentence expresses the joy and fortune of meeting a distinguished young man from a noble family.
  • This sentence describes You Mei's strategy of sending the painting and poem through a servant to see how Hui Juan responds.

  • 公信口戏答曰：“卜汝连得三鱼，计重五斤有馀。一尾烹以款客，两尾兑钱行沽。” - This sentence contains the narrator's playful response, predicting that the fisherman will catch three fish, weighing more than five pounds, and that one will be cooked to entertain guests, while the other two will be exchanged for money to buy more.
  • 果连得一鲤一鲂一鲫，鲜美可爱 - This sentence describes the actual outcome, where the fisherman catches three fish, a carp, a bream, and a crucian carp, which are fresh and delicious.
  • 渔人大喜，笑曰：“先生其神仙耶？何言之验也。寒舍不远，敢请税驾。” - This sentence contains the fisherman's reaction, expressing surprise and joy at the accuracy of the prediction, and inviting the narrator to stay at his home.
  • 公笑从之 - This sentence describes the narrator's response to the invitation, agreeing to stay.
  • 不半里，至其家，茅屋数椽，一老妪当门而坐，笑问渔人：“归何早也？” - This sentence describes the journey to the fisherman's home and the scene upon arrival, where an old woman is sitting at the door and asks the fisherman why he returned early.
  • 渔人笑曰：“儿幸遇神仙，卜应连得三鱼，果然。” - This sentence contains the fisherman's explanation to the old woman, mentioning the encounter with the narrator and the accurate prediction.
  • 因将鲤付妪，请烹以待客 - This sentence describes the fisherman giving the carp to the old woman to cook for the guest.
  • 又笑谓公曰：“此老母也，幸尚强健。年八十有二矣，齿目俱幸无损，惟重听耳。” - This sentence contains the fisherman's introduction of the old woman to the narrator, describing her age and condition.
  • 因请公少坐，自携两鱼，就邻翁兑秫酒一大瓶而归 - This sentence describes the fisherman asking the narrator to sit for a while and then going to exchange the two fish for a bottle of millet wine with a neighbor.
  • 老妪烹鱼已熟，渔人请公南向坐，老妪西向坐，自居主位北向坐 - This sentence describes the arrangement of seating during the meal, with the narrator sitting facing south, the old woman facing west, and the fisherman sitting in the host's position facing north.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '稔知前村某甲家颇小康，久病卧床，尚易为计。' is elliptical, implying that the narrator is familiar with a moderately wealthy household in a village where the head of the family is long-sick and bedridden, making it easy to plan something.
  • The long sentence '时夜漏三下，甲宅仆媪俱已睡熟，万籁寂然，予由屋一跃至地，见甲房灯尚未息，姑伏窗窥之，乃不窥则已，窥之真令人骇然也。' describes a sequence of events: it is midnight, the servants and maids are asleep, the narrator jumps down from the roof, sees the light still on in the sick man's room, peeks through the window, and is shocked by what he sees.
  • The sentence '小人惨不忍睹，复一跃上屋，恨恨而返。' describes the narrator's reaction to the shocking scene, jumping back up to the roof and returning with deep resentment.
  • The final sentence '至今思之，怒发犹为上指也。' expresses the narrator's ongoing anger and shock at the memory of the event.

  • 究不知真伪 - Means 'one cannot know the truth or falsehood,' expressing doubt about the reliability of the dream.
  • 可否不谬 - Means 'whether it is correct or not,' questioning the accuracy of the judgment.
  • 倘所梦不实 - Means 'if the dream is not true,' introducing a hypothetical scenario.
  • 为子孙者，此后不能与祭矣 - Means 'for descendants, they will not be able to participate in ancestral worship afterward,' indicating a serious consequence.
  • 兴念及此，能勿悲乎 - Means 'thinking of this, how could one not be sad,' expressing deep emotional distress.
  • 痛哭卧地，晕不能兴 - Means 'cried bitterly and fell to the ground, fainting and unable to get up,' describing a physical reaction to emotional pain.
  • 观两人别墓情形 - Means 'to see the situation of the two people separating the tomb,' referring to the scene of the dispute over the tomb.
  • 真伪是非 - Means 'truth and falsehood, right and wrong,' indicating a clear distinction between the parties.
  • 尚待吾明白宣示乎 - Means 'still need me to clearly announce it,' questioning whether further explanation is necessary.
  • 皆喻之矣 - Means 'all understand it,' indicating that the audience grasps the situation.
  • 抚心自问，尚有何说 - Means 'reflecting on oneself, what else can be said,' suggesting a self-evaluation and acknowledgment of wrongdoing.
  • 自称知罪 - Means 'admitting guilt,' indicating a confession.

  • 誓无反覆 - Means 'swearing not to change,' indicating a commitment to the decision.
  • 亲笔书判，令两姓画押 - Means 'writing the judgment by hand and having both families sign it,' formalizing the agreement.
  • 三十馀年难了葛藤 - Means 'a dispute lasting more than thirty years,' indicating a long-standing conflict.
  • 盖此山本郡丞祖墓 - Means 'this mountain is originally the tomb of the county magistrate's ancestors,' providing historical context.
  • 老诸生侦知其久失契据 - Means 'the old scholar discovered that the deed had been lost for a long time,' indicating a legal loophole.

  • 先野合生子，而后成礼 - Literally means 'first having a child through a wild union, then formalizing the marriage,' describing a sequence of events in the wedding process.

  • 《左传》所载罗人、鸗人是也 - Means 'as recorded in the *Zuo Commentary*, the Luo people and the Jiu people,' referencing a historical text.
  • 楚灭宋、蔡、罗、鸗四国 - Means 'the State of Chu conquered the four states of Song, Cai, Luo, and Jiu,' describing a historical event.
  • 俘其家室，放之南徼 - Means 'captured their families and exiled them to the southern frontier,' describing the fate of the conquered people.

  • 各有后裔，冠、婚、丧、祭、衣食 - Means 'each has descendants, with customs of capping, marriage, funerals, sacrifices, clothing, and food,' describing the cultural practices of the descendants.

  • 后鸗氏伯仲佐武侯平蛮有功 - Means 'later, the elder and younger brothers of the Jiu clan assisted Zhuge Liang in subduing the barbarians and achieved merit,' describing a historical event.
  • 兄王于滇东，去鸟为龙氏 - Means 'the elder brother became king in eastern Yunnan, changing his surname from Jiu to Long,' indicating a change in status and name.
  • 弟王于滇南，加凡为凤氏 - Means 'the younger brother became king in southern Yunnan, adding Fan to his surname to become the Feng clan,' indicating a similar change in status and name.
  • 同受爵于蜀汉 - Means 'both received titles from the Shu Han state,' indicating the political recognition of their achievements.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 里乘 - A term for local chronicles or village histories.
  • 土司 - A historical term for local chieftains or lords in China, particularly in the southwestern regions.
  • 《左传》 - The *Zuo Commentary*, an ancient Chinese text that provides commentary on the *Spring and Autumn Annals*.
  • 罗人、鸗人 - The Luo people and the Jiu people, historical groups mentioned in the *Zuo Commentary*.
  • 楚灭宋、蔡、罗、鸗四国 - A historical event where the State of Chu conquered four states, including the Luo and Jiu peoples.
  • 苗四家 - The four Miao families, referring to the descendants of the conquered peoples.
  • 周礼 - The rituals and customs of the Zhou dynasty, which influenced the cultural practices of the Earthly Lords.
  • 建子之月 - The first month of the year according to the Zhou calendar, corresponding to the month of Zi.
  • 武侯 - Zhuge Liang, a famous statesman and military strategist of the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 平蛮 - Subduing the barbarians, referring to the conquest of non-Han Chinese tribes.
  • 蜀汉 - The Shu Han state, one of the three states during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 龙氏 - The Long clan, a surname adopted by the elder brother of the Jiu clan.
  • 凤氏 - The Feng clan, a surname adopted by the younger brother of the Jiu clan.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 四家多为姻好 - Means 'the four families often formed marital alliances,' indicating a close familial relationship through marriage.
  • 嫡长女为嫡 - Refers to the eldest daughter being the legitimate heir, emphasizing her status within the family.
  • 长妇必媵八人 - Means that the eldest wife was required to have eight attendants, reflecting a traditional practice in ancient China.
  • 犹古诸侯一娶九女遗意也 - Suggests that this practice is reminiscent of the ancient custom where a prince would marry nine women, symbolizing the continuation of a historical tradition.
  • 定九先生十岁时，从季父宦滇中 - Indicates that at the age of ten, Ding Jiu followed his younger uncle to serve in Yunnan, highlighting a significant life event.
  • 逾年，季父殁 - Means that after a year, the younger uncle passed away, marking a turning point in Ding Jiu's life.
  • 随其季庶母钱、并钱之兄伯可侨寓滇中 - Describes Ding Jiu moving to live with his stepmother Qian and Qian's brother Bokai in Yunnan, emphasizing the change in his living situation.
  • 境甚窘 - Means that the circumstances were very difficult, indicating financial hardship.
  • 钱所存不上百金 - States that Qian had less than a hundred gold pieces, showing the limited resources available.
  • 交其兄伯可服贾 - Means that Qian entrusted her brother Bokai to engage in trade, suggesting a practical solution to their financial difficulties.
  • 贩售建烟 - Refers to trading in tobacco, indicating the specific commodity involved.
  • 藉得什一 - Means that they earned a tenth of the profit, highlighting the modest gains from their trade.
  • 伯可由是得与龙姓土司官交往 - Indicates that Bokai thus gained the opportunity to interact with officials of the Long family, a local chieftain, suggesting social advancement.
  • 定九幼慧，从季庶母受书 - Describes Ding Jiu as being intelligent from a young age and receiving education from his stepmother, emphasizing his early academic development.
  • 年十四通举业 - Means that at the age of fourteen, Ding Jiu became proficient in the imperial examination subjects, indicating his scholarly achievements.
  • 兼攻诗古文词 - Suggests that he also studied poetry, ancient prose, and literature, showing his broad educational interests.
  • 会诏改八股为论策 - Indicates that during a period when the imperial examination format changed from the eight-part essay to essays on policy and strategy, highlighting a significant educational reform.
  • 滇人虽老生宿儒，多不知论策体裁 - Means that even experienced scholars in Yunnan were unfamiliar with the new format, emphasizing the challenge posed by the change.
  • 知陈孺子幼而能文 - Refers to the fact that Chen Ru, a young man, was already skilled in writing, highlighting his exceptional talent.
  • 庞首皓发，皆愿执贽门下 - Describes older scholars with white hair, who all wished to become his disciples, emphasizing his growing reputation.
  • 岁可得脩脯数百金 - Means that he could earn several hundred gold pieces annually as a teacher, indicating his financial success.
  • 用遂不乏 - Suggests that his means of support were thus not lacking, showing his financial stability.
  • 定九冒滇籍，十六岁举于乡 - Indicates that Ding Jiu claimed Yunnan as his place of origin and passed the provincial examination at the age of sixteen, highlighting his academic success.
  • 初名太夏，字禹鼎 - States that he was initially named Tai Xia and given the courtesy name Yu Ding, emphasizing his early identity.
  • 因官瓯削籍 - Means that due to some official action, his registration was revoked, indicating a legal or administrative issue.
  • 乃以一字行 - Suggests that he then used a single character as his name, showing a change in his naming convention.
  • 而就军功 - Indicates that he subsequently achieved military merit, highlighting his later accomplishments.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 合卺定情始解 - Refers to the traditional Chinese wedding ceremony where the couple drinks from a shared wine cup (合卺) to seal their union. '定情' means 'to establish affection' or 'to confirm a romantic relationship,' and '始解' means 'the beginning of the resolution' or 'the start of the formalization.'
  • 復着如故 - Literally means 'to wear again as before,' implying that after the initial ceremony, the couple resumes their normal attire.
  • 生子始去 - Means 'to leave only after having a child,' indicating that the ceremony or ritual continues until the couple has a child.
  • 奏蛮乐 - Refers to playing music typical of the Miao (苗) ethnic group, emphasizing the cultural context of the wedding.
  • 苗女数十辈衣绯 - Describes Miao women dressed in red, with '绯' meaning 'bright red' or 'crimson.'
  • 击诸葛铜鼓 - Refers to the playing of the Zhu Ge bronze drum, a traditional Miao instrument, with '诸葛' possibly referring to Zhuge Liang, a famous historical figure, or simply a generic term for a large bronze drum.
  • 盘旋环绕于庭中 - Describes the Miao women moving in a circular pattern around the courtyard, symbolizing the harmony and unity of the couple.
  • 歌声如莺啼芳树 - Compares the singing to the sound of orioles chirping among fragrant trees, evoking a natural and harmonious atmosphere.
  • 以朱丝一缕系婿左臂 - Means 'to tie a red thread to the groom's left arm,' a symbolic gesture in the wedding ceremony.
  • 引丝入室，系女右臂 - Means 'to lead the thread into the room and tie it to the bride's right arm,' completing the symbolic connection between the couple.
  • 以锦蒙头 - Means 'to cover the bride's head with a brocade cloth,' a traditional practice to protect her modesty and honor.
  • 傧相赞拜 - Refers to the master of ceremonies (傧相) leading the couple in the traditional bowing ritual.
  • 如礼毕 - Means 'after the rituals are completed,' indicating the conclusion of the formal ceremony.
  • 傧相乃更锦衣舞蹈 - Means 'the master of ceremonies then changes into brocade clothing and dances,' continuing the celebratory atmosphere.
  • 请新人登车 - Means 'to invite the newlyweds to board the carriage,' marking the transition to the next phase of the wedding.
  • 举家哭送 - Means 'the entire family cries to see them off,' expressing the emotional farewell.
  • 诸媵母亦拥女泣涕登车 - Means 'the attendants and mothers also embrace the bride and cry as she boards the carriage,' emphasizing the emotional and communal aspect of the wedding.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 新郎新妇各一饮，挨递诸媵 - The groom and bride each take a sip, then pass the cup to the bridesmaids, indicating the ceremonial drinking ritual.

  • 盖苗中婴儿，最忌出痘 - Means 'in the Miao people's culture, infants most fear smallpox,' indicating a cultural belief.
  • 百无一二生者 - Means 'out of a hundred, only one or two survive,' indicating a high mortality rate.
  • 痘气又易沾染 - Means 'the smallpox virus is easily contagious,' indicating a medical fact.
  • 即壮夫染之亦无不痘，痘无不死 - Means 'even if a strong man contracts it, he will definitely get smallpox, and those with smallpox will definitely die,' indicating a grim reality.
  • 常因一儿出痘，祸延一乡，竟绝噍类 - Means 'often, the smallpox of one child can spread to an entire village, eventually wiping out all life,' indicating a devastating consequence.
  • 惟一受孕，即禁与男子接，儿生即决不出痘 - Means 'only once pregnant, they are forbidden to have sexual contact with men, and the child born will definitely not get smallpox,' indicating a preventive measure.
  • 故大家皆设室老，专司其事 - Means 'therefore, wealthy families all appoint room elders to oversee this matter,' indicating a formal practice.
  • 小户则其姑严察之 - Means 'in smaller households, the grandmother strictly monitors it,' indicating a different but equally strict practice.
  • 凡妇今夕受胎，翌晨眉间即有一缕红丝隐约现出 - Means 'any woman who conceives tonight will have a faint red thread appear between her eyebrows the next morning,' indicating a superstitious sign.
  • 大家妇人每早必参见室老，室老一见即知，曰：“若有孕矣。” - Means 'women from wealthy families must see the room elder every morning, and the room elder will know immediately, saying, 'She is pregnant,' indicating a ritualistic check.
  • 立为移榻独宿，夜必扃钥，室老日夜防闲，不少疏忽 - Means 'immediately move her to a separate bed to sleep alone, lock the door at night, and the room elder guards day and night, never neglecting,' indicating strict protection.
  • 至七月胎成，方敢解严，以关系非一人家故也 - Means 'until the seventh month when the fetus is fully formed, they dare not relax, because it concerns more than just one family,' indicating a long period of protection due to the importance of the pregnancy.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 《诗》所谓“戴星而往还”者，非欤？ - Refers to a line from the *Book of Songs* (《诗经》), indicating a reference to classical literature.
  • 两月，兰不孕 - Refers to a specific woman named Lan who was trying to conceive.
  • 龙媪又如前携花烛酒肴来，送甄姑入值 - Refers to a ritual involving a woman named Long Ao and another named Zhen Gu, indicating a cultural practice.
  • 一切如兰仪 - Refers to a standardized procedure, likely a cultural or ritualistic practice.
  • 月馀，大妇及两媵均有孕 - Refers to a successful outcome in a household, indicating a cultural context of multiple wives and concubines.
  • 盖苗中婴儿，最忌出痘 - Refers to a cultural belief in the Miao people about smallpox.

  • 痘气又易沾染 - Refers to the contagious nature of smallpox, indicating a medical context.

  • 常因一儿出痘，祸延一乡，竟绝噍类 - Refers to the devastating impact of smallpox, indicating a cultural and historical context.
  • 惟一受孕，即禁与男子接，儿生即决不出痘 - Refers to a preventive measure against smallpox, indicating a cultural and medical context.
  • 故大家皆设室老，专司其事 - Refers to a formal practice in wealthy families, indicating a cultural context.
  • 小户则其姑严察之 - Refers to a different practice in smaller households, indicating a cultural context.
  • 凡妇今夕受胎，翌晨眉间即有一缕红丝隐约现出 - Refers to a superstitious sign, indicating a cultural context.
  • 大家妇人每早必参见室老，室老一见即知，曰：“若有孕矣。” - Refers to a ritualistic check, indicating a cultural context.
  • 立为移榻独宿，夜必扃钥，室老日夜防闲，不少疏忽 - Refers to strict protection measures, indicating a cultural context.
  • 至七月胎成，方敢解严，以关系非一人家故也 - Refers to a long period of protection, indicating a cultural and social context.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 海吼 - A term used to describe the sound of the sea, which can vary in intensity and character.
  • 俗呼海叫 - A colloquial term for the sea's roar, indicating its common name among the people.
  • 乍遠乍近，若斷若連 - Describes the fluctuating nature of the sound, coming and going, sometimes breaking off and then resuming.
  • 臨流聽之，有成連鼓琴之致 - Suggests that listening to the sea's sound near the water can evoke the refined and harmonious quality of a skilled musician playing the guqin.
  • 大吼如萬馬奔騰，鉦鼓響震 - Describes the powerful and tumultuous sound of the sea, comparing it to the roar of thousands of horses galloping and the thunder of drums and gongs.
  • 三峽崩流，萬鼎共沸 - Compares the sea's roar to the combined sound of waterfalls cascading down three gorges and thousands of cauldrons boiling simultaneously.
  • 錢塘八月怒潮差可彷佛 - Refers to the famous Qiantang River tidal bore in August, which is the closest natural phenomenon to the sea's roar.
  • 觸耳駭愕 - Describes the shocking and awe-inspiring effect of the sea's sound.
  • 濡足海岸，俯瞰溟渤 - Describes the act of wading into the sea and looking out over the vast ocean.
  • 不知聲之何從出 - Expresses the mystery of where the sound originates.
  • 遠海雲氣已漸興，而風雨不旋踵至矣 - Describes the rapid onset of storms as clouds gather over the distant sea.
  • 雨徵 - A term used by people familiar with the sea to indicate that a storm is coming.
  • 冬月吼，常不雨，多主風 - States that during winter, the sea's roar often indicates wind rather than rain.
  • 天妃神 - Refers to the sea goddess, specifically the ancient Tianfei (Heavenly Consort) deity.
  • 海神，惟馬祖最靈 - Indicates that among the sea deities, the Matsu (马祖) is the most powerful and revered.
  • 凡海舶危難，有禱必應 - Describes the belief that prayers to Matsu will be answered in times of maritime danger.
  • 靈異之跡，不可枚舉 - Emphasizes the numerous miraculous signs associated with Matsu.
  • 洋中風雨晦瞑，夜黑如墨 - Describes the darkness and stormy conditions at sea.
  • 每於樯端現神燈示祐 - Describes the appearance of a divine light at the top of the mast, indicating divine protection.
  • 船中忽出爝火如燈光、升樯而滅者 - Describes the sudden appearance and disappearance of a small flame in the ship, which is believed to be a sign of impending disaster.
  • 舟師謂是馬祖火，去必遭覆敗 - Describes the belief among sailors that the appearance of the 'Matsu fire' means certain doom.
  • 船中例設馬祖棍 - Describes the practice of placing a Matsu stick on board ships.
  • 凡值大魚水怪欲近船，則以馬祖棍連擊船舷，即遁去 - Describes the ritual of using the Matsu stick to ward off sea monsters and large fish.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 水仙王者，洋中之神 - Describes the Water God as a deity of the ocean.
  • 帝禹、伍相、三闾大夫 - Names associated with the Water God, representing different historical figures who are revered as water deities.

  • 伍相浮鸱夷 - Refers to Wu Zixu, who was drowned in a bag (鸱夷) after being exiled.
  • 屈子怀石自沉 - Refers to Qu Yuan, who drowned himself in the Miluo River after being exiled.
  • 宜为水神，神爽不泯 - Suggests that these figures are appropriate as water deities because their spirits are immortal.
  • 划水仙者 - A ritual or practice of invoking the Water God for help in dire situations.
  • 海舶在大洋中，不啻太虚一尘 - Describes a ship at sea as being as insignificant as a speck of dust in the vastness of the sky.
  • 惟藉樯舵坚实，绳碇完固 - Emphasizes the importance of a sturdy mast, rudder, ropes, and anchors for the ship's safety.
  • 庶几乘波御风，乃有依赖 - Suggests that only with these elements can the ship navigate the waves and winds effectively.
  • 每遇飓风忽至，骇浪如山 - Describes the sudden onset of a typhoon and the towering waves it creates.
  • 柁折樯倾，绳断底裂 - Describes the destruction caused by the storm, including the breaking of the rudder, mast, and ropes, and the tearing of the hull.
  • 技力不得施，智巧无所用 - Indicates that even skill and intelligence are useless in such extreme conditions.
  • 惟有叩天求神，角崩稽首，以祈默宥而已 - Suggests that the only recourse is to pray to the heavens and gods, bowing and pleading for mercy.
  • 爰有水仙拯救之异 - Refers to the miraculous intervention of the Water God.
  • 余于台郡遣二舶赴鸡笼淡水 - Describes the author sending two ships to Jilong (Keelung) for fresh water.
  • 大风折舵，舶腹中裂 - Describes a strong wind breaking the rudder and splitting the ship's hull.
  • 王君云森居舟中，自分必死 - Describes Wang Yunsen, who was on board and believed he would die.
  • 惟有划水仙可免 - Suggests that only by invoking the Water God could he be saved.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 紅夷 - Refers to the Dutch, also known as 'red barbarians' due to their red hair. The term '紅夷' is a combination of '紅毛' (red hair) and '夷' (barbarian), which was a common way to describe foreigners in ancient China.
  • 大西洋附庸 - Literally means 'vassal of the Great Western Ocean,' referring to the Dutch as subjects or allies of a broader Western power.
  • 性貪狡 - Describes the Dutch as greedy and cunning.
  • 能識寶氣 - Means 'able to recognize the aura of treasure,' implying a keen sense of value or wealth.
  • 善貨殖 - Means 'skilled in trade and commerce.'
  • 重利輕生 - Means 'value profit over life,' indicating a willingness to risk life for gain.
  • 無遠不至 - Means 'no distance is too far,' emphasizing their extensive travels.
  • 夾板船 - Literally 'double-board ship,' referring to their large, sturdy ships. The term is a literal translation of '用板兩層' (using two layers of boards).
  • 圓木 - Refers to the circular timbers used in the construction of their ships.
  • 非板也 - Means 'not boards,' clarifying that the ship is not made of flat planks but rather circular timbers.
  • 巧思 - Means 'ingenious thought' or 'clever design.'
  • 帆如蛛網盤旋 - Describes their sails as resembling a spider web spiraling around, indicating a complex and efficient sail design.
  • 八面受風 - Means 'receiving wind from all eight directions,' suggesting their sails are highly versatile.
  • 無往不順 - Means 'nothing is against the wind,' implying their ships are always in favorable conditions.
  • 左右戗折 - Means 'left and right oblique folding,' describing the way Chinese sails adjust to the wind.
  • 去聲 - Indicates the tone of the character '戗' (qiāng), which is a fourth tone.
  • 因逆風從對面來 - Means 'because the wind comes from the opposite direction,' explaining why sails need to be adjusted.
  • 左右拗折 - Means 'left and right twisting,' describing the adjustments made to sails to catch the wind.
  • 以趁風力之謂也 - Means 'in order to catch the wind,' explaining the purpose of sail adjustments.
  • 欹側傾險 - Means 'leaning and dangerous,' describing the instability of Chinese ships in adverse conditions.
  • 迂回不前之艰 - Means 'difficulties of going around and not moving forward,' indicating the challenges faced by Chinese ships.
  • 不翅天垠 - Means 'not unlike the edge of the sky,' emphasizing the vastness and difficulty of the sea.
  • 恃船大帆巧 - Means 'relying on large ships and clever sails,' highlighting the Dutch advantage.
  • 常行劫盗 - Means 'often engage in piracy,' describing their frequent acts of robbery at sea.

  • 無往不順 - Suggests the superiority of Dutch ships in navigation.

  • 以趁風力之謂也 - Explains the purpose of sail adjustments.
  • 欹側傾險 - Describes the instability of Chinese ships.

  • 不翅天垠 - Emphasizes the vastness and challenges of the sea.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 使数人坐樯巅，架千里镜四面审视 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'have several people sit at the top of the mast, set up telescopes to examine all sides.'
  • 商舶虽在百里外，望见，即转柁逐之，无得脱者 - Complex sentence meaning 'merchant ships, even if they are a hundred miles away, can be seen, and immediately turn the rudder to chase them, none can escape.'
  • 常至日本贸易，日本倭知其为盗，必使中国商舶先归，计程已远，然后遣之 - Detailed description meaning 'often go to Japan for trade, the Japanese know they are pirates, so they make Chinese merchant ships return first, calculate the distance far, and then send them.'
  • 谓彼帆虽巧，然巧于逆风，反拙于乘顺 - Comparative statement meaning 'he says their sails are skillful, but skillful in going against the wind, and clumsy in going with the wind.'

  • 若与中国舟航并驰顺风中，彼反后矣 - Conditional statement meaning 'if they race with Chinese ships in a tailwind, they will fall behind.'

  • 彼既恃所长，于诸国舟航，一切易视 - Statement of arrogance meaning 'they rely on their strengths and regard the ships of other countries as easy prey.'
